title: DDL, Constraints, Schema Evolution, and Integrity Repair · Data-System Defense
summary: The support schema must reject impossible states and evolve while old application versions are still active. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a constraint-rich, reversible schema migration. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define tables with deliberate types, defaults, generated values, and named constraints.
Enforce NOT NULL, UNIQUE, CHECK, primary-key, and foreign-key invariants at the database boundary.
Choose restrict, cascade, set-null, or set-default actions from lifecycle ownership semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Integrity Repair
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Import ticket 203 twice, reassign one unowned ticket, reject an invalid severity, and migrate a required category field without breaking the old writer. Preview mutation scope and retain rollback plus changed-row evidence.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Integrity Repair.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for profile legacy violations, quarantine ambiguity, repair deterministically, and validate constraints before enforcement. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Integrity Repair, keep Integrity Repair connected to executable relational evidence.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Integrity Repair
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Integrity Repair: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Import ticket 205 twice, reassign one unowned ticket, reject an invalid severity, and migrate a required category field without breaking the old writer. Preview mutation scope and retain rollback plus changed-row evidence.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Integrity Repair.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Integrity Repair, keep Integrity Repair connected to executable relational evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Profile legacy violations, quarantine ambiguity, repair deterministically, and validate constraints before enforcement.
